title: Funimation Trailers thread
wikitext:
[]: Funimation Trailers thread.             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Dec 19, 2010 6:51 pm http://www.hulu.com/watch/201874/movie-trailers-evangelion-20-you-can-not-advance 2.0 Trailer I can't watch, unsure if dubbed or subbed because Hulu isn't available to me.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sun Dec 19, 2010 7:08 pm dammit Funi, you made the trailer dialogueless!

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Dec 19, 2010 7:29 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 19, 2010 7:48 pm Weird trailer with the background music... And whatever that shouting/voices was from that background music at the end. I wonder why they just couldn't use the original 2.0 Trailer when other countries were able to use one or two of them for festivals and such. Regardless, it's nice to actually see SOMETHING for 2.0 stateside, but I seriously wonder what they'll do regarding the title of Q. Maybe just leave it as "Q/Quickening" or perhaps Studio Kara decided on their international "Xxx xxx (not) xxxxx." title by now and will let Funimation know early before a public announcement? Or they'll publicly announce the title soon?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Dec 19, 2010 8:40 pm The sudden cut out at the end was a bit odd. I don't think it was entirely awful, but I did nearly laugh out loud at "fighting to save themselves" accompanying a clip of Rei and Asuka having a bitchfight over Shinji. And then Seele00 and I realized that having one's self being completely predicated on Shinji's affections is actually probably a pretty accurate description of 2.0, so good job, trailer. Also, the extended scene of Mari breathing heavily with her helmet on, because apparently the guy who edited the trailer together didn't feel like looking for any better dramatic or tense moments to include.

[]:             Quote Post by RMZX » Sun Dec 19, 2010 8:57 pm Filled with too many spoiler type things for people who haven't watched it in Japanese already.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Dec 19, 2010 9:34 pm RMZX wrote: Filled with too many spoiler type things for people who haven't watched it in Japanese already.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Dec 19, 2010 10:30 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Honestly one of the worst trailers I've ever seen. That music was shit, not even from the movie I'm almost certain, sounded like the kind of Midi that's too obviously Midi aside from the vocals. The way the video just ends sucked too. I think any and every one of us with a day and a nice program could have cut together a better trailer, maybe even *gasp* using a song from the film. Fucking shit.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 19, 2010 10:44 pm The trailer was ridiculous. I laughed out loud at the way they played the slap, and the dubbed-in yell after Mari's breathing would have made me laugh if I weren't so confused. Yes, the trailer definitely had spoilers. Watching it, I can tell that the Zeruel-ish Angel turns Rei-ish, that the NERV pyramid is obliterated by one energy blast from the Zeruel-ish Angel, and that Unit 01 turns red. Those were three of the most shocking (in a good way) elements for me when I watched the camrip and seeing those out of context beforehand would have ruined that initial feeling. Well, yeah, watching it as a camrip first may not have been the smartest idea, but it was so exciting watching it as a camrip first that I probably would have passed out had I seen it for the first time in a theater.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Mon Dec 20, 2010 2:26 am So Hulu decided "Hey lets make trailers viewable all over the world!" all of a sudden and now I can watch the trailer. It's a decent trailer. Lots of crazy going on just like in the movie. I did like the tape player start and it's abrupt end was tape player stop (and I think I heard it rewinding, volume was low). It's neat.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 20, 2010 2:31 am Hatsumi92 wrote: So Hulu decided "Hey lets make trailers viewable all over the world!"

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Mon Dec 20, 2010 3:37 am oh GOD!!! That trailer....funimation what the hell did you guys do? The background music was just atrocious and the editing? Tried to be overdramatic? Cuz the end result failed horribly! Give us a freakin' trailer with dub voices and we'll be happy. Thanks. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Dec 20, 2010 8:44 am This looks like something Funi have thrown out quickly because they havent finished the sub yet.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Dec 20, 2010 12:21 pm Hmm, Mari's panting is actually dubbed. What's more, you can hear a little scream when she's starting up Unit-05. I suppose that it's from another cut, given that she was narrating Unit-05's activation instead of yelling. I wonder if that's her actual dub voice, or did they just ask ome random female employee to pant and scream a little as a placeholder.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Mon Dec 20, 2010 12:34 pm Jayfive wrote: This looks like something Funi have thrown out quickly because they havent finished the sub yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Dec 20, 2010 12:59 pm Sorry I meant dub, not sub. No idea why i put sub. Either way, if this is the format for Funi trailers normally then fair enough. Im still approaching the treatment in the dub with some trepidation that Funi will stuff it up - i.e. Mari gets a ridiculous accent, they make Kaji sound like an uber-relaxed surfer dood, Grant turns everything up to 11 and Asuka sounds like a cross between Lucy from Peanuts and Violet Elizabeth Bott, Spencer buggers up the final scene by drowning out 'Tsubasa...' too much and/or by not yet learning how to scream without it sounding silly or finally (and i believe this is a particular party trick of US anime dubs) play narration or other speech over quiet/silent scenes. I dont want them to stuff it up, I actually like the dub of 1.0 but i cant say im not a bit worried. But that said I like to think that if they nail it, 2.0 could be this generation's Akira for the english-speaking world or do something equivalent to 'Spirited Away'. Maybe thats overdoing it, but we can dream

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Mon Dec 20, 2010 1:31 pm Jayfive wrote: I dont want them to stuff it up, I actually like the dub of 1.0 but i cant say im not a bit worried. But that said I like to think that if they nail it, 2.0 could be this generation's Akira for the english-speaking world or do something equivalent to 'Spirited Away' . Maybe thats overdoing it, but we can dream

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Mon Dec 20, 2010 1:45 pm It looked fine to me. It's pretty much like all Funimation trailers. I think you guys don't watch enough anime trailers. Or trailers in general. They're meant to entice the audience.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Dec 20, 2010 1:49 pm I was waiting for something like that regarding my last post Ive only been aware of the show for a couple of months, pardon my (over)-enthusiasm. Its just nice not to be the cynical old fart about something for a change And anyway - im talking about the show standing up to those other two in terms of quality. Im talking its intrusion into the consciousness of the wider public, not just the anime geeks. Let me put it this way, Spirited Away (along with a few other Ghiblis) and Akira have both been shown on UK non-specialised TV and few other anime films have, i can imagine 2.0 appearing on at least Film Four, if not Channel 4.

[]: In Japan on the other hand...             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Mon Dec 20, 2010 1:59 pm Jayfive wrote: I was waiting for something like that regarding my last post Ive only been aware of the show for a couple of months, pardon my (over)-enthusiasm. Its just nice not to be the cynical old fart about something for a change And anyway - im talking about the show standing up to those other two in terms of quality. Im talking its intrusion into the consciousness of the wider public, not just the anime geeks. Let me put it this way, Spirited Away (along with a few other Ghiblis) and Akira have both been shown on UK non-specialised TV and few other anime films have, i can imagine 2.0 appearing on at least Film Four, if not Channel 4.
